words: caused by pioneering. Back in my family, there were three sisters who

speaker: CG
words: lived together in a cabin in the woods. And in the spring one day, two

speaker: CG
words: of the sisters were out cleaning up the winter fall from the trees and

speaker: CG
words: so on, cleaning up around the house outside. And one of the sisters was

speaker: CG
words: in the house. It happened that she was the smallest of the three, but

speaker: CG
words: she was in the house and the other two cleaning up outside and one

speaker: CG
words: of them looked up from her raking outside and she said, "Sis-Hirs a

speaker: CG
words: bear;" And she and the sister that were outside ran in, and the sister

speaker: CG
words: that was inside grabbed the door and let them in and then she slammed

speaker: CG
words: the door and the three of them put their weight on the door and they

speaker: CG
words: had it with the bear's head in and his neck caught in the door. Well,

speaker: CG
words: this youngest sister happened to be the most expert, the little one-,

speaker: CG
words: she wasn't little because she was young, she was just the smallest— but

speaker: CG
words: being the smallest even then she was the most expert with an axe cr

speaker: CG
words: any of the sisters. So the two heavier sisters held the door, and of

speaker: CG
words: course, they kept the axe in the house, where would they keep it?— they

speaker: CG
words: had nothing but the house— she grabbed the axe and she chopped the bear's

speaker: CG
words: hear until the bear fell dead, and then the sisters fell down with ex

speaker: CG
words: haustion! Well, anyway, they had a bearskin. They could make mocassins

speaker: CG
words: or they could make a rug or whatever it was they needed. And, although

speaker: CG
words: it was in the spring, and the bear was almost meatless from it's winter

speaker: CG
words: hibernation, but still it would make soup, at least. So they were in!

speaker: CG
words: And they didn't think anything of that, it was just what came up and

speaker: CG
words: they had to do it, and they did it.

speaker: SS
words: Was that extremely rare for a woman or women to pioneer by themselves?

speaker: CG
words: No. It wasn't rare at all for a woman to be alone a long time. Now

speaker: CG
words: whether there was any one else who lived in this cabin at any time, I

speaker: CG
words: never heard. I just heard this much. But for a woman to be alone endlessly

speaker: CG
words: while her husband was gone to market a hundred miles away, or

speaker: CG
words: was hunting or whatever—

speaker: SS
words: Maybe out getting work someplace else.

speaker: CG
words: There an incident in our own family. The man— now when I say he was a

speaker: CG
words: hunter, I'm saying the same thing as though I had said he was a doctorhe

speaker: CG
words: was a blacksmith, he was anything else. A hunter was a man who pro

speaker: CG
words: fessionally was a hunter, and he made his living that way and provided

speaker: CG
words: skins and meat on a cash basis. Well, this man was a hunter and he would

speaker: CG
words: be gone sometimes long time on a hunt. And he was gone a long time

speaker: CG
words: and when he would go home— when he would get nearly home, close to homehe

speaker: CG
words: would go cautiously until he would get in sight of the house to see

speaker: CG
words: whether he had a house left-there or whether the Indians had burned it

speaker: CG
words: down, and they might still be around the fire. Well, he was going cau

speaker: CG
words: tiously the last way through the timber and he got where he could see

speaker: CG
words: and here was his wife and the three children, the youngest a baby in

speaker: CG
words: arms, and one of two years or so old, and one older than that, and his

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: and she had the baby in her arms and the two year old behind the saddle

speaker: CG
words: and the younger one standing on the block with her with the baby in her

speaker: CG
words: arms, and she was trying to get on with the baby in her arms and get the

speaker: CG
words: boy on the block up behind the saddle with the other child, but the

speaker: CG
words: horse, with anyone on the block would not go near it. It would keep

speaker: CG
words: circuling back away from the block, and she couldn't reach the one there,

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: and put the baby down on the block and put the older boy on behind the

speaker: CG
words: little one and got on and thought she'd pick up the baby, but the horse

speaker: CG
words: would not go to the block with the baby lying on it. And she was trying

speaker: CG
words: every maneuver she could make with the three children, and her husband

speaker: CG
words: watched her quite a while, and finally he showed himself and he said,

speaker: CG
words: "Wife, what are you trying to do?" She said, "I've stayed he along

speaker: CG
words: with the bear and the deer and the panther and the rest of them as long

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: He said, "Well, let's unsaddle the horse and put her away and I'll be

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: There's a story that you told me when I was here before that we didn't

speaker: CG
words: take down and it was a good one. And that was the story about a stage

speaker: CG
words: coach that was caught in a storm. How does that story go?

speaker: CG
words: Well, it was in Montana earlier than the days I was there, and I was

speaker: CG
words: there when it was quite early. But, I saw the house where the people

speaker: CG
words: went to and I don't know that it was the same family there or not. But

speaker: CG
words: this stage was going from Harlem to Lewistown, which is quite a distance,

speaker: CG
words: and a blizzard overtook the stage and the man said he would go to this

speaker: CG
words: house, which he could still see, and see if they could stay overnight.

speaker: CG
words: Well, at that time in that country you did not ask to stay overnight,

speaker: CG
words: you went and they said, "Have you had supper, yet, we are just going

speaker: CG
words: to sit down, sit down with us, and we'll fix you a bed." You didn't

speaker: CG
words: ask, they offered. But he went in and explained that the blizzard had

speaker: CG
words: overtaken them just outside here, and he had a coach of people and

speaker: CG
words: could they come in and stay til the blizzard was over? They said, no,

speaker: CG
words: they didn't take people in. Well, here, they just weren't going to take

speaker: CG
words: him in, that's all there was to it. So, he went on thinking he could

speaker: CG
words: get to some other place, or something. And one of his horses fell dead.

speaker: CG
words: He had a coach of men and a twelve year old girl, and as soon as the

speaker: CG
words: horse fell dead, he took all the blankets that were available, 70 he

speaker: CG
words: had in the coach and put them down by the horse, while it was still warm

speaker: CG
words: and bedded the twelve year old girl there and covered her all up and

speaker: CG
words: then he had the men all circle in a line and all clasp hands and they

speaker: CG
words: circuled around that horse with the little girl lying by it and when it

speaker: CG
words: would get to be whatever he thought might be an hour or so he would tell

speaker: CG
words: them that now they could stop for five minutes, but not let go of hands

speaker: CG
words: and stand right where they were, and he would see about the little girl

speaker: CG
words: that she was all right. And she was keeping warm enough with the blan

speaker: CG
words: kets and had that warmth to start with, and she was asleep and then he

speaker: CG
words: would get into the circle with the men and, "Now, we'll circle again

speaker: CG
words: for about an hour and then we'll stop five minutes again." And they

speaker: CG
words: kept that up until morning and the storm was over.

speaker: CG
words: And they told quite a story about Mrs. Winters. She believed in ob

speaker: CG
words: serving the Sabbath. They were ranchers— well, he was a cattleman out

speaker: CG
words: on Beaver Creek. And he wanted and did, always wait until Sunday for

speaker: CG
words: his dehorning and branding and so forth, because the other men were avail

speaker: CG
words: able on Sunday. Well, I don't know why they couldn't be available on

speaker: CG
words: any other day, but they weren't. But, anyway, he did all of that on

speaker: CG
words: Sunday and she tried to talk him out of it— that the Sabbath was the

speaker: CG
words: Lord's Day and it was not for that purpose. But, anyway, he'd say.^'I

speaker: CG
words: can get the other men here and I need help." So he kept right on with

speaker: CG
words: it. So finally, one Sunday with a bible in her hand climbed to the top

speaker: CG
words: rail of the corral— she didn't say a word to the men at all— she just

speaker: CG
words: climbed to the top rail of the corral and she sat there reading aloud,

speaker: CG
words: paying no attention to the men at all, just reading aloud. (Chuckles)

speaker: CG
words: Well, after a littlejOne of the men got on his horse and rode away.

speaker: CG
words: Pretty soon another man got on his horse and rode away. Well, they quit

speaker: CG
words: their work right then! She never said a word about it at all.(Chuckles)

speaker: CG
words: They used to tell that story about it, and for a long time I had the

speaker: CG
words: sewing machine, I bought it from Mrs. Winters, the sewing machine that

speaker: CG
words: she'd always had. I don't know whether there were any more sewing mach

speaker: CG
words: ines in the country or not, but I had it for years.

words: to Giltedge? He said, "Yes, you get in the stage and I'll take you. It'll

speaker: CG
words: it's a group of women banded together for the protection of the home

speaker: CG

speaker: CG

speaker: CG

words: tories so weary, hopeless and she decided that there should be a law

words: to stop it. And she got it put over. And at one time, we had what we

speaker: CG
words: liquors. And it was signed by women in several countries of the world.

speaker: CG

speaker: SS

speaker: SS
words: this.

words: pasted the paper with them on on a roll of muslin and it made a roll

words: couldn't carry it in alone he had to have a man help him with it, so

speaker: CG

words: a day he'd had quite a lot of them; if he got a hundred that was stup

speaker: CG
words: ly and he was getting piled under with letters. He called the national

speaker: CG
words: president and he said to her, "Madam, if you will call off your women

speaker: CG
words: I will give you anything that you askl" So that is what a petition will

speaker: CG

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: United States put a push on Congress, Congress would be compelled to do

speaker: CG
words: something about it. We could stop it and have it lowered to what would

speaker: CG
words: be more reasonable.

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: certificate in North Dakota. You see at that time, when you started

speaker: CG

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: certificate in South Dakota, two certificates there, third grade and

speaker: CG
words: then the second grade. And not being college I never had a first grade

words: which was life. And the first grade certificate was very rare. There

speaker: CG
words: was just almost nobody that had the first grade certificate^ There

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: tificates and taught in Montana. And that was just a marvel to people,

speaker: CG
words: they'd speak about, "Oh, yes, but you know, she has a first grade."

words: It was a matter of comment! And then in Montana I had to get a Montana

speaker: CG

speaker: CG

speaker: CG

words: to school, when you can go to school to. Gibbs. Having taught for

words: seven years. I knew whether he was teaching or whether he wasn't!

speaker: CG
words: Oh, he was good. He did make one mistake one time. "Well," he said,

speaker: CG
words: "I shouldn't have said that, I should have waited for you to say it."

speaker: CG
words: And went right on just as though he hadn't made a mistake. That's the

speaker: CG
words: only mistake he made the whole semester. And, he knew precisely what

speaker: CG
words: he had for us $vy that time, and he told us precisely what we would have

speaker: CG
words: the next time. We noticed that so much because we'd had— well, I'm

speaker: CG
words: sure that the professor that we had— I had all the time before that—

speaker: CG
words: was a relation that had to be taken care of, so somebody that was in

speaker: CG
words: good here got him in here for a couple of years.

speaker: SS
words: Did you feel that you learned a great deal when you were going to school

speaker: SS
words: then?

speaker: CG
words: I felt that I received— I wouldn't say learned, I'd say received— a

speaker: CG
words: great deal. Maybe I can express it this way; I cooked on the campus

speaker: CG
words: for ten years, and I cooked for six years for a place where they had no

speaker: CG
words: room for me, so'd I'd come home for the afternoon and go back and get

speaker: CG
words: the dinner and come home for the night. And in the short days when

speaker: CG
words: the campus would be lighted when I'd come home, I'd stand on the top of

speaker: CG
words: Sixth Street hill and I'd say, "I own part of that." One woman said

speaker: CG
words: to me, "I'd like to know how you own any part of it." And I said, "Well

speaker: CG
words: you do too, you pay taxes and so do I," I said, "anybody that pays tax

speaker: CG
words: es owns part of the University." But I'd stand there and say, "I own

speaker: CG
words: part of that." But after I was a student I would stand on the top of

speaker: CG
words: the Sixth Street hill and say, "I am a part of that.1' And that was

speaker: CG
words: not met with anywhere else. And it made a distinct difference in me.

speaker: CG
words: If you would known me before, I think you'd see it.

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: too easily shifted by the current. But it hardened me in a way that has

speaker: CG
words: been very beneficial to me. And being a part of it, I was somebody ill S

speaker: CG
words: that I never had been before. Not that I lost what I had been before,

speaker: CG
words: but merely added this. It was really a very high adventure for me. AS

speaker: CG
words: far as learning was concerned, the first three semesters with this young

speaker: CG
words: professor— I wouldAsay,vI lear^much of anything. And I didn t

speaker: CG
words: acquire as much, although I was acquiring it. One day one girl stopped

speaker: CG
words: me at the foot of the steps and she said to me, "Well, why are you doing

speaker: CG
words: this? Is it for what you get out of it?" "Well, do you do anything

speaker: CG
words: for what you get out of it?" I told her, yes, that was why I was doing

speaker: CG
words: it! And went on my way laughing at such a question. (Chuckles) I read

speaker: CG
words: would go to college. Well, when he graduated from Coll^Q^ . ., he had

speaker: CG
words: a wealthy uncle, and this uncle said, "I'll give you a thousand dollars

speaker: CG
words: a year as long as you go to college. If you'll go the full year I'll

speaker: CG
words: give you a thousand dollars." Well, at that time a thousand dollars was

speaker: CG
words: a fortune. If the head of a family had a thousand dollars anywhere he

speaker: CG
words: was rich.

speaker: CG
words: So this boy thought, "Huh, I could pay the college expenses

speaker: CG
words: and I could have a good time and I'll have a thousand dollars when I

speaker: CG
words: get through, and won't I have a good time then!" So, he went to college.

speaker: CG
words: He got all taken up with it and he decided, well, two thousand'd be bet

speaker: CG
words: ter than one, he'd go another year. And he went on until in the '70's

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: college because they want to. They get from it something that

speaker: CG
words: is not to be had anywhere else. There are lots more things that you

speaker: CG
words: can't get anywhere but in that thing, like a certain profession$and so

speaker: CG
words: on, but if you hate college you don't get the college atmosphere inbuilt

speaker: CG
words: into you. And in the two years, well, I knew I was changed, I wasn't

speaker: CG

speaker: CG
words: determined to go three years and probably four, but I got down

speaker: CG
words: and couldn't get up, and that was the end of it. (Chuckles) I had fun

speaker: CG
